page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 1 thread per core. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the ecs.g6a.large sits in the lower performance tiers for intensive computational tasks. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both scored 766.59, placing the instance in the bottom 10% of benchmarked servers. Similarly, the PassMark CPU score of 2792.75 and database operations score of 689.47 fall into the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor performance, with read-only large block bandwidth at 23.06 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 15.78 GB/sec. However, single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show average performance, with gzip compression reaching 23.75 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression achieving 9.2 GB/sec.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and modest hardware profile, this instance offers an entry-level option for workloads that do not require high computational density or high-speed local storage. The lack of bundled local storage means users must rely on network-attached storage options. The ecs.g6a.large is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, such as basic web serving, single-threaded file compression tasks, small-scale development environments, or low-traffic application hosting where high CPU or memory throughput is not critical.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz on the KVM hypervisor. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows poor to weak performance in CPU-intensive and memory-bound tasks, with stress-ng and PassMark scores falling in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested servers. However, it demonstrates average performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level, non-intensive workloads. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, small development environments, and basic application hosting.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core AMD EPYC virtual server with balanced memory for entry-level hosting and lightweight application workloads.

8.0 GB of system memory with 4.0 GB per core
